# building-agentskills

> Layered authoring patterns for AI agent skills; cross-platform spec docs, the three-question framework, mechanism-vs-decoration, anti-patterns, and ship-evidenced case studies.

## Quickstart

You are an AI agent reading this file to orient yourself in the building-agentskills project. The project is documentation plus a thin loader skill, not a runtime library.

Read three docs first, in this exact order, before any other section of this site or repo.

They are the load-bearing context; everything downstream assumes you have already absorbed them.

Start at the [overview](/docs/00-overview) for the layered architecture. It clarifies what this repo adds on top of the platform spec and what it inherits without restating.

Continue to the [quickstart](/docs/01-quickstart) for a zero-to-working-SKILL walkthrough you can complete end to end in roughly ten minutes.

Finish at the [mental model](/docs/02-mental-model) to separate skills from CLAUDE.md, hooks, and slash commands so you pick the right primitive for the job at hand.

After those three, the rest of the site (frameworks, anti-patterns, case studies, ship-evidenced retrospectives) is safe to consult in any order driven by the current task.

<!-- TOOLS:START -->
(auto-generated; do not edit manually. Run `npm run build:llms`)

### Hero

- [Overview: the layered architecture](docs/00-overview): This repo is the third layer of a stack. Two layers below it already exist; their conventions and constraints carry forward. This page names the layers, what each adds, and what each leaves out, so yo...
- [Quickstart: your first skill in 10 minutes](docs/01-quickstart): This page walks you from zero to a working SKILL.md that activates in Claude Code on your machine. It is the shortest path to "the agent picked up my skill"; it deliberately does not cover anti-patter...
- [Mental model: skills vs CLAUDE.md vs hooks vs slash commands](docs/02-mental-model): This is the decision matrix. Before you write a skill, ask: is a skill actually the right primitive for what you want? The cost of a wrong-primitive answer is a skill that never triggers, that bloats...
- [The three-question framework](docs/03-three-questions): Authoring a skill well requires answering three questions. They are the hero mental model for this repo. Every other doc anchors back to one of them.
- [Token economics: the why of skill constraints](docs/04-token-economics): This is Question 3 of the hero framework (`docs/03-three-questions.md`). What is the token budget for your skill, and what governs it?

### Authoring

- [Frontmatter: the field-by-field reference](docs/05-authoring/frontmatter): This is the exhaustive reference for SKILL.md frontmatter, organized as two columns: cross-platform-safe (Layer 1, agent-skills spec) and Claude Code extensions (Layer 1 spec compliance preserved; Lay...
- [Prose discipline: voice and snippet-as-code rules](docs/05-authoring/prose-discipline): This page covers two distinct concerns that look like one. First, the voice and conventions for SKILL.md prose (Layer 2 superpowers conventions, mostly). Second, the silent correctness bug class that...
- [Triggers: description as activation contract](docs/05-authoring/triggers): The description is the only part of your SKILL.md the agent reads on every turn. It is the activation contract. If the description does not name a trigger, the skill never fires for that trigger. This...
- [Iron Laws and the four canonical discipline-prose patterns](docs/05-authoring/iron-laws): This page covers the patterns superpowers added on top of the agent-skills spec for discipline-shaped skills. Layer 2 conventions, all four. None of them are required by the spec; all four are observa...
- [Line budget: why 500 lines is a contract](docs/05-authoring/line-budget): The 500-line cap on SKILL.md is not a stylistic preference. It is a load-bearing constraint tied to Claude Code's auto-compaction floor. This page explains the why; the numerical detail is in `docs/04...

### Testing

- [RED-GREEN-REFACTOR for prose changes](docs/06-testing/red-green-for-prose): This page covers the discipline for SKILL.md prose changes. The standard RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle (Layer 2 superpowers convention from test-driven-development) maps cleanly to script changes; it does...
- [Unit tests: what to test in a skill](docs/06-testing/unit-tests): A SKILL.md is documentation. Most documentation is not testable. Skills are different: their scripts are testable like any other code, their disciplines are testable via pressure scenarios, and their...
- [Tests that pass immediately: the two valid TDD inversions](docs/06-testing/tests-that-pass-immediately): Standard TDD discipline (Layer 2 superpowers convention from `test-driven-development`): write the test, watch it fail (the RED step), then write the code to make it pass (the GREEN step). The skill's...

### Standalone

- [Mechanism vs decoration: the strongest single signal](docs/07-mechanism-vs-decoration): Every threshold, invariant, or rule in your SKILL.md is either decoration or mechanism. Decoration is fine for guidance prose. For invariants (words like "must," "always," "never," numeric thresholds)...
- [Packaging as a plugin](docs/08-packaging-as-plugin): This page covers Claude Code plugin packaging from the karpathy-wiki shape: minimal `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, skills in `skills/<name>/SKILL.md`, optional sibling sub-directories, the symlink inst...
- [Evolution: ship cycles, semver, and deprecation](docs/09-evolution): Skills evolve. The audit-driven hardening cycle that produced karpathy-wiki v2.2 is the same cycle this repo expects authors to use for their own skills. This page covers the cycle, how to version a s...
- [Anti-patterns: failure modes catalog](docs/10-anti-patterns): Each anti-pattern below has a one-line definition, a one-line evidence trail (citing the karpathy-wiki v2.2 commit or the source report section), and a one-line counter. Use this as a checklist for se...
- [Update mechanism: how new lessons flow in](docs/12-update-mechanism): This page covers how new lessons enter this repo. v0 ships ONE case study; this file describes how subsequent ones land. The mechanism has three sources: per-ship retrospective, reader-submitted issue...

### Cross-platform

- [Claude Code: harness-specific frontmatter and hooks](docs/11-cross-platform/claude-code): This page covers Claude Code-specific behaviors, frontmatter extensions, and patterns that do not apply to other harnesses. The cross-platform-safe core lives in the rest of the docs; this page is for...
- [Codex: agents/openai.yaml sidecar](docs/11-cross-platform/codex): OpenAI's Codex CLI (`openai/codex`) supports the agent-skills format natively, plus an optional Codex-specific sidecar manifest for OpenAI-side extensions. This page covers what is portable and what i...
- [Gemini CLI: Extensions, not Skills](docs/11-cross-platform/gemini-cli): Google's Gemini CLI does NOT support Agent Skills. It uses a different mechanism called Extensions. Authors trying to ship the same skill on Gemini cannot do so directly; they must port to the Extensi...
- [Other harnesses: OpenCode, Cursor, Hermes, Continue.dev, Copilot CLI](docs/11-cross-platform/others): This page covers the remaining harnesses in the agent-skill ecosystem as of mid-2026. Each gets one paragraph: what it supports, what it does not, and where it diverges from the cross-platform-safe co...

### Case studies and examples

- [Case study: karpathy-wiki v2.2 ship](case-studies/2026-04-25-karpathy-wiki-v2.2): This is the seed case study for `toolboxmd/building-agentskills`. It documents what one stateful, artifact-manipulating skill learned in shipping v2.2: what worked, what failed, what the existing cano...
- [Minimal Skill](examples/minimal-skill/SKILL): A working minimal SKILL.md the quickstart references. Copy this file as your starting point; replace the frontmatter and body with your own.
